---
title: 'Tongues | The Facts'
date: '2015-04-23T04:15:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '886560684128341390'
blogger_filename: '/2015/04/tongues-facts.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Darrell Wolfe, Storyteller/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Spiritual Growth'
---

<br /><br /><br /><br /><table align="center" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="tr-caption-container" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto; text-align: center;"><tbody><tr><td><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/all-rights-reserved-lion-tongue-by-doug-wheller-505f8f8391f6.jpg" style="margin-left: auto; margin-right: auto;"><img alt="All rights reserved &quot;Lion tongue&quot; by Doug Wheller       1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant" border="0" height="320" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/all-rights-reserved-lion-tongue-by-doug-wheller-505f8f8391f6.jpg" title="All rights reserved &quot;Lion tongue&quot; by Doug Wheller      1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant" width="305" /></a></td></tr><tr><td class="tr-caption" style="font-size: 12.8px;"><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin: 0in;">All rights reserved&nbsp;<a href="https://flic.kr/p/feCWYU" target="_blank">"Lion tongue" by Doug Wheller</a></div></td></tr></tbody></table><u><span xmlns=""></span><br /></u><br /><u><br /></u><br /><br /><br /><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-foundation.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Foundation</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-foreshadowing.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Foreshadowing</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-fulfillment.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Fulfillment</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-facts.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Facts</a></li><li><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2015/04/tongues-familiar.html" target="_blank">Tongues | The Familiar</a></li><div style="-webkit-text-stroke-width: 0px; color: black; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-size: medium; font-style: normal; font-variant: normal; font-weight: normal; letter-spacing: normal; line-height: normal; margin: 0px; orphans: auto; text-align: start; text-indent: 0px; text-transform: none; white-space: normal; widows: 1; word-spacing: 0px;"></div><br /><br /><br /><div style="border-width: 100%; direction: ltr;"><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.0861in;"><div style="direction: ltr; margin-left: 0in; margin-top: 0in; width: 6.0861in;"><div style="font-size: 18pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white; font-weight: bold;">Tongues What's that about?</span></div><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-corinthians/12.html"><span style="background: white; font-size: 12pt; font-style: italic;">1 Corinthians 12</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Signs Follow Believers</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">The Holy Spirit has been, since day one, intimately involved with Mankind. He is the one hovering over us and ready to move at a momentâ€™s notice. Now let's take a look at a few things regarding Tongues in the New Testament and cover some basics.&nbsp;</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/mark/passage.aspx?q=mark+16:17-19"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Mark 16:17-19</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">17&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">And these signs will follow those who believe</span><span style="font-style: italic;">: In My name they will cast out demons;&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">they will speak with new tongues</span><span style="font-style: italic;">; 18 they will take up serpents; and if they drink anything deadly, it will by no means hurt them; they will lay hands on the sick, and they will recover." 19 So then, after the Lord had spoken to them, He was received up into heaven, and sat down at the right hand of God.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">In&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/mark/16-17.html">Mark 16:17</a>&nbsp;Jesus tells the disciples that those who would believe the message of the Gospel would speak with New Tongues among other things. This is not optional. So we see from this scripture, and others in a moment, that tongues is for every believer. These signs WILL follow those who believe. This doesn't mean that those who don't speak in tongues are not believers. This simply means that it is the right and responsibility of believers to&nbsp;utilize&nbsp;this gift.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Supernatural Event -&nbsp;</span><span style="font-weight: bold; text-decoration: underline;">Unknown&nbsp;</span><span style="font-weight: bold;">Tongues</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/acts/2.html"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Acts 2</span></a><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt;">&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">6 And when this sound occurred, the multitude came together, and were confused, because everyone heard them speak in his own language. 7 Then they were all amazed and marveled, saying to one another, "Look, are not all these who speak Galileans? 8 And how is it that we hear,&nbsp;</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">each in our own language</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">&nbsp;in which we were born?</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">Of course we've seen that in&nbsp;</span><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/acts/2.html"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">Acts 2</span></a><span style="background: white;">&nbsp;when the Holy Spirit came to earth the very first evidence that he had arrived was a fireworks display in the house and public display of tongues. There are some important things to look at in this first account though.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">Many people believe that tongues is simply the ability to speak in a known earth language, without having learned the language. They would call a language that cannot be understood gibberish. If this understanding of Tongues were true, it would follow that of the 120 disciples speaking to the crowd that day, different ones were speaking in different known languages so that all the languages present were being spoken by all the speakers so that all the hearers would have representation. Therefore there were no strange sounds or incoherent sounds, it was just many languages of the known people present. Many who hold this belief also say that all tongues are this way.&nbsp;They point to Acts Chapter 2 as the evidence for this claim.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">My problem with that interpretation lies on two fronts. </span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><ul style="direction: ltr; margin-bottom: 0in; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0in; unicode-bidi: embed;" type="disc"><li style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-top: 0px; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt;">First      from a logical point of view, there would be no need for a "Gift of      Interpretation" if this were true, because all one would need is a      person present who spoke that language. If the gift of Tongues was merely      a tool for reaching the other lands where the speaker didn't know the      language then no "Gift" would be required for interpretation,      the hearers would understand&nbsp;perfectly. However since 1 Corinthians      12 clearly states that there is a gift of interpretation and Paul goes on      to say that if everyone spoke in tongues in a service than no body would      understand anything, it seems clear this isn't a semi-natural event, but a      totally supernatural one. </span></li></ul><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">These are not merely known languages.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><ul style="direction: ltr; margin-bottom: 0in; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0in; unicode-bidi: embed;" type="disc"><li style="margin-bottom: 0px; margin-top: 0px; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt;">Second,      although tongues can be manifested as known languages not learned by the      speaker, more on that later, this is not supported by Acts 2.&nbsp;A      closer look at Acts 2 shows a different scenario. In Acts 2 we see that      there are 17 or so different people groups mentioned. Each having      languages and dialects. Some could have cross-over since speaking only one      language is really unique to the USA. Many regions had multiple languages      or dialects per region. So there may have been plus or minus of 17 languages      present, probably more.&nbsp;</span></li></ul><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">Have you ever heard many people of one language all speaking at once? It's confusing enough in and of itself. Multiple languages all speaking at once would be even harder to hear. That would be quite a chaotic scene.&nbsp;The text of this verse plainly tells us that the people heard the disciples "EACH IN OUR OWN LANGUAGE."&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">This paints a different scenario than that above. This paints that each person, heard the entire group, in his/her language. A Persian heard the WHOLE GROUP in Persian. A Greek heard the WHOLE GROUP in Greek. This not only fits the text more accurately, but it fits the God of Order who created the event. God is a God of order. Disorder and&nbsp;Chaos&nbsp;is not of God. This was addressed by Paul later who said that if there were a Tongue given to the group as a message it should be done in order, one at a time. In this case, all spoke, but they spoke and were heard, as one.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-weight: bold;">As Prayer Language and&nbsp;Unknown&nbsp;Tongue</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">The Bible Speaks of Tongues as a Prayer Language. Tongues is an unknown language. It is often called gibberish by those who don't understand what's going on. This is usually by unbelievers, or believers who just don't get it. It's often confused with the devil's&nbsp;counterfeit&nbsp;in witchcraft. More on that later.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-corinthians/14.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">1 Corinthians 14</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">2&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">For he who speaks in a tongue does not speak to men but to God, for no one understands him; however, in the spirit he speaks mysteries.&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic;">3 But he who prophesies speaks edification and exhortation and comfort to men. 4 He who speaks in a tongue edifies himself, but he who prophesies edifies the church.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">14 For if I pray in a tongue, my spirit prays, but my understanding is unfruitful. 15 What is the conclusion then?&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">I will pray with the spirit</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, and I will also pray with the understanding. I will sing with the spirit, and I will also sing with the understanding. 16 Otherwise, if you bless with the spirit, how will he who occupies the place of the uninformed say "Amen" at your giving of thanks, since he does not understand what you say? 17 For you indeed give thanks well, but the other is not edified. 18 I thank my God I speak with tongues more than you all; 19 yet in the church I would rather speak five words with my understanding, that I may teach others also, than ten thousand words in a tongue.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">When you speak with and/or pray with Tongues, you are praying "In the Spirit". You are speaking with and to God, not to men. Your spirit is praying words as prompted by The Holy Spirit. This is&nbsp;referred&nbsp;to again in several places. Ephesians tells us that after being dressed in the armor of God we should pray in Tongues.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/ephesians/6-18.html"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Ephesians 6:18</span></a></div><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">18 And pray in the Spirit on all occasions with all kinds of prayers and requests. With this in mind, be alert and always keep on praying for all the saints.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">Jude tells us that we are to build ourselves up on our most holy faith, and pray in Tongues. Why is it our most holy faith? Because the words you pray with in Tongues are given by the Holy Spirit. Your mind cannot mess the prayer up with fear, doubt, unbelief, and un-biblical ideas.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/jude/1-20.html"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Jude 1:20</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in 0in 0in 0.375in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">20 But you, dear friends, build yourselves up in your most holy faith and pray in the Holy Spirit.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Paul tells us in Romans that we are not without help in our prayer. When we pray, there are often times we just don't know what to pray about, or what to pray. If you are faced with a situation in which you don't know what to pray about, or what the will of God is in that situation, you should pray in Tongues. The Spirit of God prays through us and even though WE don't understand what He's praying through us, we can be sure that He is making intercession and working all things to our good.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/romans/passage.aspx?q=romans+8:26-28"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Romans 8:26-28</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">26 Likewise the Spirit also helps in our weaknesses. For we do not know what we should pray for as we ought, but the Spirit Himself makes intercession for us with groanings which cannot be uttered. 27 Now He who searches the hearts knows what the mind of the Spirit is, because He makes intercession for the saints according to the will of God. 28 And we know that all things work together for good to those who love God, to those who are the called according to His purpose.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">As A Sign, not THE sign, but a sign. </span></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/acts/passage.aspx?q=acts+10:41-48"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Acts 10:41-48</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">44 While Peter was still speaking these words, the Holy Spirit fell upon all those who heard the word. 45 And those of the circumcision who believed were astonished, as many as came with Peter, because the&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">gift of the Holy Spirit</span><span style="font-style: italic;">&nbsp;had been poured out on the Gentiles also. 46&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">For they heard them speak with tongues and magnify God</span><span style="font-style: italic;">. Then Peter answered, 47 "Can anyone forbid water, that these should not be baptized who have received the Holy Spirit just as we have?"</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Peter is invited to&nbsp;Cornelius'&nbsp;house, a gentile worshiper of God. Peter preaches Jesus to the Gentiles for the first time. Peter, and his crew, are shocked to see that the Holy Spirit fell upon them too! Notice what is said here: "For they heard them speaking with tongues..." Tongues were the evidence to Peter that these men had been filled with the Holy Spirit.&nbsp;</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/acts/passage.aspx?q=acts+19:1-11"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Acts 19:1-11</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">1 And it happened, while Apollos was at Corinth, that Paul, having passed through the upper regions, came to Ephesus. And finding some disciples 2 he said to them, "Did you receive the Holy Spirit when you believed?" So they said to him, "We have not so much as heard whether there is a Holy Spirit." 3 And he said to them, "Into what then were you baptized?" So they said, "Into John's baptism." 4 Then Paul said, "John indeed baptized with a baptism of repentance, saying to the people that they should believe on Him who would come after him, that is, on Christ Jesus." 5 When they heard this, they were baptized in the name of the Lord Jesus. 6&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">And when Paul had laid hands on them, the Holy Spirit came upon them, and they spoke with tongues and prophesied.&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic;">7 Now the men were about twelve in all.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Later Paul comes across a group of men who believed in the Messiah, but they were baptized into John's Baptism. They hadn't heard of Jesus or the Holy Spirit yet. They were saved! Just hadn't been filled yet. Then Paul lays hands on them and they are filled with the Holy Spirit. What is the result? They speak in tongues and prophecy.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Tongues are also called a sign in 1 Corinthians 14. It is said that Tongues are a sign for unbelievers. This refers back to Acts 2 when people were astonished by what they heard. If however, there is no supernatural interpretation, the people who are unbelievers would be confused by what they heard and consider the group out of their mind, which was also a reaction by certain people in Acts 2.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-corinthians/14.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">1 Corinthians 14</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">22 Therefore tongues are for a sign, not to those who believe but to unbelievers; but prophesying is not for unbelievers but for those who believe. 23 Therefore if the whole church comes together in one place, and all speak with tongues, and there come in those who are uninformed or unbelievers, will they not say that you are out of your mind? 24 But if all prophesy, and an unbeliever or an uninformed person comes in, he is convinced by all, he is convicted by all. 25 And thus the secrets of his heart are revealed; and so, falling down on his face, he will worship God and report that God is truly among you.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-weight: bold;">Separate</span><span style="font-weight: bold;">&nbsp;from New Birth</span></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/john/3.html"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt;">John 3</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">3 In reply Jesus declared, "I tell you the truth, no one can see the kingdom of God unless he is&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">born again</span><span style="font-style: italic;">. " 4 "How can a man be born when he is old?" Nicodemus asked. "Surely he cannot enter a second time into his mother's womb to be born!" 5 Jesus answered, "I tell you the truth, no one can enter the kingdom of God unless he is born of water and the Spirit. 6 Flesh gives birth to flesh, but the&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">Spirit gives birth to spirit.</span><span style="font-style: italic;">&nbsp;7 You should not be surprised at my saying, 'You must be born again.' 8 The wind blows wherever it pleases. You hear its sound, but you cannot tell where it comes from or where it is going.&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">So it is with everyone born of the Spirit.</span><span style="font-style: italic;">"</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="margin-left: .375in; margin: 0in;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/john/passage.aspx?q=john+20:17-27"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">John 20:17-27</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">19 On the evening of that first day of the week, when the disciples were together, with the doors locked for fear of the Jews, Jesus came and stood among them and said, "Peace be with you!" 20 After he said this, he showed them his hands and side. The disciples were overjoyed when they saw the Lord. 21 Again Jesus said, "Peace be with you! As the Father has sent me, I am sending you." 22 And with that he breathed on them and said, "</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">Receive the Holy Spirit</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">. 23 If you forgive anyone his sins, they are forgiven; if you do not forgive them, they are not forgiven."</span>&nbsp;</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/acts/1.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Acts 1</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">4 On one occasion, while he was eating with them, he gave them this command: "Do not leave Jerusalem, but&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">wait for the gift my Father promised</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, which you have heard me speak about. 5 For John baptized with water, but in a few days</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">&nbsp;you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit."</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">8</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">&nbsp;But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you</span><span style="font-style: italic;">; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth."</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">The "New Birth" is when our spirit has been created new by the Holy Spirit. THAT is the point that we&nbsp;receive&nbsp;the Holy Spirit. He doesn't just come in and re-create us and leave.&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/search/?q=lives+in+us&amp;c=&amp;t=niv&amp;ps=10&amp;s=Bibles">He lives in us</a>. We know we are born again by His Spirit&nbsp;<a href="http://bible.cc/1_john/3-24.htm">1 John 3</a>. The same Spirit who raised Jesus from the dead dwells in us!&nbsp;<a href="http://bible.cc/romans/8-11.htm">Romans 8</a>.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">We see in John 20, Jesus comes to His disciples and breathes on them and they receive the Holy Spirit. They are born again at THAT moment, in a room with Jesus present. BUT Jesus tells them to go and wait until the Holy Spirit comes upon them in Acts 1, which He does in Acts 2. Now wait a minute...</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">How can Jesus give them the Holy Spirit in a room in John 20, and then tell them to wait for the Holy Spirit in Acts 1? Is the scripture contradicting itself? Are these two conflicting stories about how the Holy Spirit came? Are there two Holy Spirits? No. These are two different events both describing the same Holy Spirit. One event is that which got them Born Again, just like the men Paul ran across in Acts 19. The other event describes the FILLING of the Holy Spirit, which brings power to be witnesses, which is where the signs come from in Mark 16. This event is&nbsp;separate&nbsp;but also important. We see the original disciples got it in two rounds. The men in Acts 19 got it in two rounds. But the men in Acts 10, with Peter, got it in one round, at the same time.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">So it's possible to be born again and spirit filled&nbsp;simultaneously&nbsp;and it's possible to have these occur as&nbsp;separate&nbsp;events.</span><span style="font-style: italic;">&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/acts/8.html"><span style="background: white; font-size: 13.5pt;">Acts 8</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">12 But when&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">they believed Philip as he preached the good news of the kingdom of God and the name of Jesus Christ, they were baptized</span><span style="font-style: italic;">, both men and women. 13 Simon himself believed and was baptized. And he followed Philip everywhere, astonished by the great signs and miracles he saw. 14 When the apostles in Jerusalem heard that Samaria had accepted the word of God, they sent&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">Peter and John to them. 15 When they arrived, they prayed for them that they might receive the Holy Spirit, 16 because the Holy Spirit had not yet come upon any of them</span><span style="font-style: italic;">; they had simply been baptized into the name of the Lord Jesus. 17&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">Then Peter and John placed their hands on them, and they received the Holy Spirit. 18 When Simon saw&nbsp;</span><span style="font-style: italic;">that the Spirit was given at the laying on of the apostles' hands, he offered them money...</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="background: white;">We see this a third time in Acts 8. Philip is in Samaria preaching Christ there. The men of the city believed in Jesus and were baptized in water. These men,&nbsp;scripturally, are born again. Yet John and Peter come down to check out this event and the Holy Spirit had not yet come upon any of them. So we see here that there is something missing that gave the Apostles CLEAR indication that the Holy Spirit had not fallen on ANY of them. We also see that Simon the sorcerer SAW something that he felt was worth paying for! So although Tongues was not directly mentioned here, it's clear that a sign was involved, from all the other accounts of the Holy Spirit coming on people, its clear that Tongues was the evidence they lacked and then saw.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">So a few things are obvious from these scriptures:</span></div><ul style="direction: ltr; margin-bottom: 0in; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0in; unicode-bidi: embed;" type="disc"><li style="margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">The Filling of the Holy Spirit is NOT the same as      being born again.</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">The Filling of the Holy Spirit is NOT required to BE      born again.&nbsp;</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">The Filling of the Holy Spirit is      NOTICEABLY&nbsp;different and easily evident.</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">The Filling of the Holy Spirit is&nbsp;desirable, and      something believers should have, otherwise the Apostles wouldn't have      bothered to lay hands on them for it, and Jesus wouldn't have told the      disciples to go and wait for it before going out as witnesses.&nbsp;</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">The Holy Spirit filling can come by simply believing</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">The Holy Spirit filling can come by the laying on of      hands (which we also saw in the Old&nbsp;Testament&nbsp;several times in      Part 1)</span></li><li style="margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt; vertical-align: middle;"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">The Filling of the Holy Spirit, should also be a more      than one time event (Be Ye Being Filled Continually)</span></li></ul><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">For everyone</span></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/1-corinthians/12.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">1 Corinthians 12</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">1 Now concerning spiritual gifts, brethren, I do not want you to be ignorant</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">4 There are diversities of gifts,&nbsp;</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">but the same Spirit.</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">&nbsp;5 There are differences of ministries, but the&nbsp;</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">same Lord.</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">&nbsp;6 And there are diversities of activities, but it is the&nbsp;</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">same God who works all in all.</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">&nbsp;7 But the</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic; font-weight: bold;">&nbsp;manifestation of the Spirit is given to each one for the profit of all:</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">This is by far one of the least taught aspects of 1 Corinthians 12. Of all the things there is to say about the gifts of the Spirit This deserves an extra few moments. First of all, these gifts do NOT belong to you. These gifts listed in 1 Corinthians 12 are manifestations of the Holy Spirit. In other words these are HIS gifts to His body.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">When the Holy Spirit shows up with physically noticeable presence, these are the types of things that will occur. If these things are not&nbsp;occurring&nbsp;in your Church, the Holy Spirit is not showing up there. He may be there, He's everywhere. He may want to show up in a physical way. If the most you allow Him to do is give you a few Goosebumps during an "afterglow service", He'll take that opportunity. He's always there, always wanting to participate in a service and in a life. But those Goosebumps are the least possible physical showing up He can do. These gifts are His primary modus operandi (method of operation).</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Also, notice that it's all one. The Holy Spirit, The Son, and The Father are all mentioned here, because when they show up in the fullness of their trinity they are here to do one thing: Remove Burdens and Destroy Yokes. Jesus said that is what the Anointing is here to do, and the Holy Spirit IS the Anointing. </div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">But this is the part I've never heard anyone teach on, they may be teaching on it, but I've never heard it.&nbsp;<span style="font-weight: bold;">GOD WORKS ALL IN ALL.</span>&nbsp;That settles ALL debate for me.&nbsp;<span style="font-weight: bold;">All means "ALL" and that's all "ALL" means.</span>&nbsp;God works all these gifts in all people as He wills. He works all in all. End of debate. All people can and should operate in all of the gifts. Period.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">However, as this is specifically addressing how things should operate IN A CHURCH SERVICE, not everyone can or should do all these things all the time every time. So He will move upon certain people at certain times in public situations. Typically He will move upon those who have been praying and preparing themselves spiritually and mentally to be used. The guy who shows up 20 minutes late to service every Sunday and doesn't pray&nbsp;throughout&nbsp;the week probably won't be the one used. The gifts in a personâ€™s life outside the Church walls are a different subject.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Paul goes on to say, in 1 Cor 12:31, that we SHOULD seek and desire the BEST gifts. What are the BEST gifts? They are the gifts that are best for the occasion. If you come&nbsp;across&nbsp;a man bleeding to death on the sidewalk,&nbsp;prophesying&nbsp;to him may not be the best gift for the situation. Jesus promised you in Mark 16 that anyone who believes would be able to lay hands on the sick and they would recover. So lay hands on that man and expect God to move. If you are in a Church service, prophecy may be a more appropriate gift. Or maybe it would be helpful when talking to someone at work that asks for your advice to operate in a Word of Knowledge or Wisdom? Tongues, more often than not, are for personal use in prayer.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Anointing for Everyone</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Jesus promised the Holy Spirit to us. He said that it would be BETTER that He go to heaven, because we'd get this gift of The Holy Spirit. What a great statement! If the Holy Spirit is more useful to me on this earth than Jesus, I want to know why. Don't you?</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/john/16.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">John 16</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">12 I still have many things to say to you, but you cannot bear them now. 13 However, when He, the Spirit of truth, has come, He will guide you into all truth; for He will not speak on His own authority, but whatever He hears He will speak; and He will tell you things to come. 14 He will glorify Me, for He will take of what is Mine and declare it to you. 15 All things that the Father has are Mine. Therefore I said that He will take of Mine and declare it to you.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/john/passage.aspx?q=john+7:37-39"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">John 7:37-39</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">37 On the last day, that great day of the feast, Jesus stood and cried out, saying, "If anyone thirsts, let him come to Me and drink. 38 He who believes in Me, as the Scripture has said, out of his heart will flow rivers of living water." 39 But this He spoke concerning the Spirit, whom those believing in Him would receive; for the Holy Spirit was not yet given, because Jesus was not yet glorified.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">So then the Holy Spirit is here to comfort and guide us and lead us into truth that we were not able to receive while Jesus was still here. Why weren't we able to receive? Jesus hadn't died, therefore we weren't born again, therefore we were still spiritually dead. The Holy Spirit in us makes us alive again. That same Holy Spirit that raised Jesus' body from the dead lives inside of us.&nbsp;<a href="http://bible.cc/romans/8-11.htm">Romans 8:11</a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/luke/11-13.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Luke 11:13</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">13 If you then, being evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your heavenly Father give the Holy Spirit to those who ask Him!"</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Our Father, who loves us enough to send his own Son to die for us, took it another step closer and wants to give us The Holy Spirit. This is more important to our livelihood than bread or water. Now that's saying something.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/acts/passage.aspx?q=acts+2:38-39"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Acts 2:38-39</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">38 Then Peter said to them, "Repent, and let every one of you be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ for the remission of sins; and you shall receive the gift of the Holy Spirit. 39 For the promise is to you and to your children, and to all who are afar off, as many as the Lord our God will call."</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">The promise of the Holy Spirit is for everyone who believes. You don't believe, don't worry, you He won't bother you. It's for those who believe. &nbsp;</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/luke/4-18.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Luke 4:18</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">18 "The Spirit of the Lord is upon Me, Because He has anointed Me To preach the gospel to the poor; He has sent Me to heal the brokenhearted, To proclaim liberty to the captives And recovery of sight to the blind, To set at liberty those who are oppressed;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;">&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/isaiah/61.html"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">Isaiah 61</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;">&nbsp;<span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">1 "The Spirit of the Lord God is upon Me, Because the Lord has anointed Me To preach good tidings to the poor; He has sent Me to heal the brokenhearted, To proclaim liberty to the captives, And the opening of the prison to those who are bound;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;">&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/isaiah/10.html"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">Isaiah 10</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;">&nbsp;<span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">27 It shall come to pass in that day That his burden will be taken away from your shoulder, And his yoke from your neck, And the yoke will be destroyed because of the anointing oil.</span></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/matthew/passage.aspx?q=matthew+3:10-17"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt; font-style: italic;">Matthew 3:10-17</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;">&nbsp;<span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">16 When He had been baptized, Jesus came up immediately from the water; and behold, the heavens were opened to Him, and He saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting upon Him. 17 And suddenly a voice came from heaven, saying, "This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased."</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;">&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/matthew/4-1.html"><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">Matthew 4:1</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;">&nbsp;<span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">1 Then Jesus was led up by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">We see that Jesus was baptized and after received the Holy Spirit which was His anointing for Ministry work. He did his first miracles afterward, in&nbsp;<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/john/2-11.html">John 2:11</a>. He was led by the Spirit afterward. He preached about His Anointing afterward. So we too, should be Anointed of the Holy Spirit, and then afterward do the works He's called us to do.&nbsp;<span style="background: white;">We see that Jesus did what he did because He was anointed of the Holy Spirit.&nbsp;</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 12pt; margin-bottom: 10pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-weight: bold;">Received By Faith</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">This is important. Faith is required for everything. Without Faith it is impossible to please God. (<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/hebrews/11-6.html">Heb 11:6</a>) Jesus, in His hometown, was unable to do miracles because of&nbsp; unbelief. The Son of God was unable to do mighty works in because of unbelief. False religious doctrines and unbelief will prevent you from receiving the Holy Spirit. Religiously trained people have a harder time with this than those who have no Church upbringing. (<a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/nkjv/mark/passage.aspx?q=mark+6:1-10">Mark 6:1-10</a>) We receive the Holy Spirit, like we&nbsp;receive&nbsp;ALL of God's gifts to us, by and through Faith. Faith is an active force, not a simple belief. If you don't already know that Faith is a spiritual tool by which we access all the promises of God, I invite you to check out this two part&nbsp;article&nbsp;from&nbsp;<a href="http://kcm.org/">KCM.ORG</a>. Understanding Faith&nbsp;<a href="http://www.kcm.org/real-help/article/understanding-faith-part-1-%E2%80%93-if-god-cant-do-anything-without-faith%E2%80%A6neither-can-we">part 1</a>&nbsp;and &nbsp;<a href="http://www.kcm.org/real-help/article/understanding-faith-part-2-%E2%80%93-framing-your-world-faith">part 2</a>.&nbsp;</div><div style="font-family: Calibri; font-size: 11pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><a href="http://www.biblestudytools.com/galatians/3-14.html"><span style="font-size: 13.5pt;">Galatians 3:14</span></a></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-left: 0.375in; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">&nbsp;</span><span style="background: white; font-style: italic;">4 He redeemed us in order that the blessing given to Abraham might come to the Gentiles through Christ Jesus, so that by faith we might receive the promise of the Spirit.</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">In my case, I came from a deeply religious background, with lot's of things to UN-learn. The baptism of the Holy Spirit didn't just fall on me. I went to multiple people to lay hand on me to&nbsp;receive, but they didn't have it either as far as I know now, so it didn't do much for me. That is, nothing happened right away. But I was able to use that laying on of hands as a point of mental contact for my faith.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">As I recall, I drove my truck out to a patch of dirt where no-body would bother me. I got in the back under the stars and prayed for a little bit.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><span style="font-style: italic;">I said: "Lord, I'm convinced by your Word that Tongues is real and that I received the Holy Spirit when hands were laid on me. I feel crazy and silly, but I am going to speak in Tongues now and you're going to help me."</span></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin-bottom: 5pt; margin-top: 0pt;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">Well, nothing came for a moment. But then I saw these words floating to the top of my mind, it felt as though they came from my belly. I chose to speak them. More came. It wasn't long before a whole language developed, now days it's several languages depending on my mood and the mood of the Holy Spirit. Now I hear someone saying: "You can't just choose to speak, you've got to be led." Well yes and no. The bible says in&nbsp;<a href="http://bible.cc/1_corinthians/14-32.htm">1 Cor 14:32</a>&nbsp;when referring to the gifts of the Spirit, that the spirit of the prophet is subject to the prophet. This statement was issued in direct refute to the idea that being led by the Holy Spirit makes you unable to control yourself or be in control of the situation. We yield to Him, yes. We are the one who chooses to speak or be quite though. He's always ready to talk to you, he's just waiting for you to speak. More on prayer Tongues later.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;">I've heard as many stories about how and when it came as I've met people who use it. Tongues are as personal to prayer as fingerprints are to hands. It's an amazing gift. Many people who are waiting on God to give them this gift are waiting in vain. God gave them the gift 2,000 years ago. They only need take it.</div><div style="font-size: 13.5pt; margin: 0in;"><br /></div></div></div></div>Until Then #<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a>?<br /><br /><br />*<br /><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>Tweet These:</b><br />#<a href="http://ctt.ec/8E5X7" target="_blank">TellYourStory</a>#<a href="http://ctt.ec/5xc67" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a></blockquote><br />More about&nbsp;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">BeStillBeLed</a>&nbsp;&lt;<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/2014/10/be-still-and-be-led-by-his-still-small.html" target="_blank">here</a>&gt;<br /><h4>And now I leave you with a question:</h4><blockquote class="tr_bq"><b>So... what are your thoughts about this? What did this make you think about?&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank"></a></b></blockquote><br /><b>Hit me up on Twitter&nbsp;<a href="https://twitter.com/darrellwolfe" target="_blank">@DarrellWolfe&nbsp;</a>or on Google&nbsp;<a class="g-profile" href="https://plus.google.com/106337514196493406365" target="_blank">+DarrellWolfe</a></b><br /><h4><g:plus action="share"></g:plus></h4><h4>By&nbsp;<a href="https://plus.google.com/+DarrellWolfe/posts" target="_blank">Darrell Wolfe</a></h4><div><ul><li><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/about-20.html" target="_blank">New Here? Start HERE</a></span></li><li><span style="font-family: &quot;georgia&quot; , &quot;times new roman&quot; , serif;"><a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/got-freedom.html" target="_blank">Got LIFE?</a></span></li><li><a href="http://eepurl.com/MzAVr" target="_blank">Insight In Your Inbox: Sign up for email delivery here</a>.&nbsp;</li><li>Get My Book: "<a href="http://www.darrellwolfe.com/p/banking-basics-for-believers-book-by.html" target="_blank">Banking Basics for Believers</a>", which guides you through the basics of banking and money from a Christian Perspective.&nbsp;</li></ul></div><div class="separator" style="clear: both; text-align: center;"><a href="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" style="margin-left: 1em; margin-right: 1em;"><img border="0" height="232" src="/images/imported/blogger-googleusercontent-com/cover-art-e0f7cc956c34.png" width="320" /></a></div>
